campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss and vote on updating the business license tax rates that companies pay to operate in the city. Finance department staff presented the proposed changes to these fees, and council members debated the matter in public before making a final decision.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased or adjusted
Annual update to fees that local businesses must pay
Finance department presented the specific rate changes
Council will vote on whether to approve the new rates

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could voice their opinions on a proposed new rule or law. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward with introducing the ordinance and skipped the standard first reading requirement, planning to vote on adopting it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from the previous session and review the city's finances for February. The council also approved several construction contracts and authorized the Town Manager to sign agreements for building projects and professional services.
keyTopics: Approved February 2026 financial report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other vendors
Approved multiple professional service agreements
Confirmed previous meeting minutes from April 7, 2026

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve meeting minutes and discuss several infrastructure and service projects. Main items included planning for a new elevator at the Terminal, setting up cleanup services for homeless encampments, updating a digital advertising agreement with Clear Channel, and approving a memorial event for fallen firefighters.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project
Encampment Cleanup Services proposals
Digital Signage Agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor
Light the Night memorial event for fallen firefighters

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and heard from residents during public comment. The main focus was deciding which neighborhood improvement projects should get funding for the next fiscal year, with council members discussing and recommending grants to support community initiatives.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Neighborhood grant funding decisions for fiscal year 2026-27
Review of neighborhood meeting attendance
Committee reports and staff updates

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for open positions on city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to provide feedback either in person or online during the public comment period.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period (in-person and online options)
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a private negotiation meeting about buying or selling a property on Finch Avenue. City leaders Tina Kapoor and Floy Andrews discussed the deal's price and payment terms with the Cupertino Union School District. The council also reviewed a program called Silicon Valley Hopper and talked about getting grant money for it.
keyTopics: Property deal at 10480 Finch Avenue between city and school district
Negotiating price and payment terms for the property
Silicon Valley Hopper program and future grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also approved a new employment agreement with the city's firefighters union covering 2026-2029.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs approved for Levi's Stadium area during World Cup 2026
New three-year contract agreement with Santa Clara firefighters (Local 1171)
One agenda item was withdrawn before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas city leaders held a meeting to approve plans for how the city will spend money on big projects over the next five years (2027-2031) and reviewed the budget for the upcoming fiscal year. They also adopted a master plan for new parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to how the City Council operates.
keyTopics: Approved 5-year capital spending plan for 2027-2031
Reviewed FY 2026-27 operating budget proposal
Adopted Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
Updated City Council rules and procedures handbook

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss several items including leadership elections and a major development proposal at 4103 Old Trace Road. The main focus was a request to subdivide an empty 1-acre lot into 9 residential lots that would allow for 9 new single-family homes plus 7 junior accessory dwelling units (smaller secondary homes on the properties).
keyTopics: Subdivision proposal at 4103 Old Trace Road to create 9 new homes
Addition of 7 junior accessory dwelling units to increase housing
Election of new City Council Chair and Vice Chair
Project classified as environmentally exempt under state law
Development in Residential Estate zoning district
